title: corylus urticifolia
intro: Corylus urticifolia, also known as the stinging or nettle-leaved hazel, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots possess medicinal properties, primarily due to their content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Corylus urticifolia include its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and folk medicines.
